metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC3 — e.g. `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.replay --fixture tests/fixtures/adjudication_68.jsonl | python scripts/check_routing_delta.py --expect-blocked COH,E2,F1,G5,G6,T5e --expect-posture-promoted G1G2,T1,T8,E4 --expect-unchanged E1,T3,A1,T4` — and specify the expected output artifact so done-ness is independently reproducible.
findings.finding: AC3 ('offline replay confirms the six lowered criteria now block their previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings, and no criterion outside the approved set changes posture') names a claimed outcome but provides no concrete proving command or script — 'offline replay' is not a runnable artifact, leaving this criterion unmeasured.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `scripts/check_routing_delta.py` (or equivalent) that loads the new `criteria_routing.json`, runs the fixture findings through the router, and asserts: (a) all 0.60-0.69 band findings for COH/E2/F1/G5/G6/T5e now resolve to `block`, and (b) criteria outside {COH, E2, F1, G5, G6, T5e, G1G2, T1, T8, E4} have unchanged `block_threshold` and `default_posture` versus the prior committed config. Reference this script in AC3 as the proving command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'six currently-advisory criteria' count in Why with the explicit promotion list in What (either name the sixth criterion or correct the count to match the four/five listed).
findings.finding: The 'Why' section states 'six currently-advisory criteria scored 75-100% block-worthy' to justify promotions, but the 'What' section lists only G1G2, T1, T8, E4 as promoted — which is four or five criteria (depending on whether G1G2 is one entry or two). An implementer reading the Why may conclude a sixth criterion should also be promoted, creating ambiguity about whether the What list is complete.
findings.location: Why section ('six currently-advisory criteria') vs What section ('Promote to default_posture: blocking: G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75')
findings.suggested_fix: If G1G2 is a combined entry representing two criteria (G1 and G2), state that explicitly (e.g. 'Promote G1, G2, T1, T8, E4 — five criteria'). If a sixth criterion was intended, name it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the criteria_routing.json changes (e.g., 'revert the JSON edit and re-run tests' or a feature-flag approach), satisfying T4's reversibility requirement.
findings.finding: The plan changes criteria_routing.json in a way that will cause previously-passing plan reviews to be blocked (a breaking behavioral change for all plan-review consumers), but provides no explicit rollback or back-out path. The offline replay in AC3 is a pre-deployment validation step, not a reversibility mechanism.
findings.location: Scope section and Acceptance Criteria AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If the new thresholds cause unacceptable false-positive blocks, revert criteria_routing.json to its prior state (git revert or restore from backup) and re-run the unit test suite to confirm restoration. The offline replay dataset should be retained to support re-adjudication.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend the replay AC) to verify that promoting G1G2, T1, T8, and E4 to blocking posture does not cause unintended mass-blocking of the existing plan corpus, and acknowledge any downstream CI/agent workflows that currently rely on these criteria being advisory.
findings.finding: The four criteria promoted to blocking posture (G1G2, T1, T8, E4) have no replay or impact-check AC verifying their effect on the existing plan corpus, and the plan does not acknowledge downstream consumers (e.g., CI gates, agent workflows) that currently treat these criteria as advisory and will now receive blocking verdicts.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet; 'What' section (promote to blocking list)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the replay AC: 'An offline replay against the existing plan corpus confirms that the four newly-blocking criteria (G1G2, T1, T8, E4) block only the intended finding classes and that the false-positive rate remains within acceptable bounds; any CI/agent integration points are notified of the posture change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, E4 @0.75' means set `block_threshold` to those values (in addition to `default_posture: blocking`), or leave `block_threshold` at 0.95 — state the intended final values for both fields explicitly for all four criteria (a small table suffices).
findings.finding: The plan's promotion notation 'G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75' is ambiguous: it is unclear whether '@0.70'/'@0.75' means only change default_posture to blocking (leaving block_threshold at 0.95), or also lower block_threshold to 0.70/0.75. The live file shows all four at block_threshold=0.95. The AC references 'the table above (per-criterion block_threshold + default_posture)' but no explicit table exists — only the prose bullet. This leaves the executor without a ground-truth for the required block_threshold values of these four criteria.
findings.location: What section — 'Promote to `default_posture: blocking`: G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75' and AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a two-column table to the What section: criterion | block_threshold | default_posture, with final target values for all 10 affected criteria, so the AC parity check and test updates have an unambiguous spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether per-criterion block_threshold/default_posture tests for the ten named criteria already exist; if not, rephrase the AC from 'updated' to 'written' and scope the test-authoring work explicitly.
findings.finding: The AC states 'Plan-review unit tests updated and green' (implying pre-existing tests that assert per-criterion block_threshold/default_posture), but a full search of the test suite found NO tests that assert specific block_threshold or default_posture values for any of the ten criteria named in the plan (COH, E2, F1, G5, G6, T5e, G1G2, T1, T8, E4). The only threshold-asserting plan-review test found (test_removal_rationale_advisory_and_coaching) checks a different criterion. If these tests don't yet exist, the plan should say 'written' not 'updated', and the scope of work is larger than described.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 2: 'Plan-review unit tests updated and green'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the offline replay mechanism exists (name the script/command/dataset) and add a pointer to it in the AC, or — if it requires building — scope that work explicitly.
findings.finding: AC line 3 asserts 'An offline replay confirms the six lowered criteria now block their previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings.' No offline replay harness, script, or documented mechanism was found in the codebase. This is a committed AC that rests on an unverifiable capability claim about a tool that cannot be confirmed to exist.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the What section (or add an explicit table) whether G1G2, T1, T8, and E4 also receive a lowered `block_threshold` (0.70 / 0.75 respectively), not just a `default_posture: blocking` change; the AC currently references a non-existent 'table above'.
findings.finding: The 'Promote to blocking' table entries for G1G2, T1, T8 (each '@ 0.70') and E4 ('@ 0.75') are ambiguous about whether the `block_threshold` value itself changes (from the current 0.95 to 0.70/0.75), or only `default_posture` changes while the threshold stays at 0.95. The AC says 'criteria_routing.json matches the table above (per-criterion block_threshold + default_posture)' but no table is provided — the plan text is the only specification, and the '@' notation is not defined.
findings.location: ## What — 'Promote to `default_posture: blocking`: G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75' / AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit two-column table in ## What (or ## Acceptance Criteria) listing the exact `block_threshold` and `default_posture` values for every changed criterion, mirroring the format the AC already promises.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either point AC3 at an existing replay/batch-runner invocation command (with its name and invocation flags), or replace it with a verifiable offline check (e.g. 'run `pytest tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py -k routing` and assert the six criteria now route findings at confidence ≥0.60 to block').
findings.finding: AC3 requires 'an offline replay confirms the six lowered criteria now block their previously-stranded 0.60–0.69 band findings' — but no such replay tool, script, or named mechanism exists anywhere in the codebase. The AC is unverifiable as written because the verification step names a non-existent artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly acknowledge in the Scope section that per-criterion threshold/posture tests for the changed criteria are net-new (not updates to existing tests), and enumerate the test file(s) and assertion pattern to be added.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Plan-review unit tests updated' but no existing unit test in the repository asserts per-criterion `block_threshold` or `default_posture` values for the criteria being changed (COH, E2, F1, G5, G6, T5e, G1G2, T1, T8, E4). The only routing-value assertions found are for `removal-rationale` (advisory) and `hedge` (exec:1-TURN). Adding threshold/posture assertions for the changed criteria would be net-new test code (CREATION), which the plan does not explicitly scope.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'Edit `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` and the plan-review unit tests that assert per-criterion `block_threshold`/`default_posture`'
findings.suggested_fix: In test_plan_review_evals.py (or a new test function), add assertions of the form: `assert routing['G1G2']['block_threshold'] == 0.70 and routing['G1G2']['default_posture'] == 'blocking'` for each changed criterion, mirroring the existing pattern at line 265 for removal-rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the plan explaining why a direct `criteria_routing.json` edit (with no engine changes) is the complete and correct mechanism — e.g. that this file is already the sole source of truth for `block_threshold`/`default_posture` consumed by the routing engine, and the engine is already parameterized by these fields.
findings.finding: The plan gives strong empirical rationale for WHAT to change but omits any positive rationale for WHY the chosen mechanism (direct hand-edit of criteria_routing.json, no engine changes) is the right approach — e.g. that criteria_routing.json is already the sole declared source of truth for per-criterion threshold/posture consumed by the routing engine, and that no engine change is needed because the existing consumer is already parameterized by those fields.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the 'What' or 'Scope' section, e.g.: 'This is sufficient because `criteria_routing.json` is the sole source of truth for per-criterion `block_threshold` and `default_posture` already consumed by `registry.threshold_for` and `_any_blocking_criterion`; the routing engine requires no changes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate a concrete proving command or script with AC3 — name the fixture file (e.g. `tests/fixtures/adjudication_68.jsonl`), the invocation (e.g. `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.replay --fixture … --expect-blocked COH,E2,F1,G5,G6,T5e --expect-promoted G1G2,T1,T8,E4`), and the expected output format so AC3 is independently reproducible.
findings.finding: AC3 references 'an offline replay' as the verification protocol for the most safety-critical acceptance criterion, but no such script, CLI invocation, fixture file, or expected output format is defined anywhere in the plan or the repo. This is a processing protocol/decision rule referenced but never co-located with the artifact that needs it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which test file (e.g. `tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py` or a new `test_routing_thresholds.py`) and what assertion form to use for each changed criterion — e.g. `assert routing['T8']['block_threshold'] == 0.70 and routing['T8']['default_posture'] == 'blocking'` — so AC2 has a deterministic, reviewable completion criterion.
findings.finding: The plan says unit tests assert 'per-criterion `block_threshold`/`default_posture`' for the changed criteria, but no existing test in the suite pins these values for T8, G1G2, T1, or E4 (the four promoted criteria). The plan does not specify what test file to update, what the assertion form should look like, or whether to add new test functions vs. extend existing ones — leaving the executor to invent the test structure without a protocol.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parametrized test in `tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py` or `test_plan_review_evals.py`: `@pytest.mark.parametrize('cid,threshold,posture', [('COH',0.60,'blocking'),('E2',0.60,'blocking'),('F1',0.60,'blocking'),('G5',0.60,'blocking'),('G6',0.60,'blocking'),('T5e',0.60,'blocking'),('T4',0.70,'blocking'),('G1G2',0.70,'blocking'),('T1',0.70,'blocking'),('T8',0.70,'blocking'),('E4',0.75,'blocking')])` asserting the routing values match the approved table.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
